task: Write a Python function second_largest(nums) returning the second largest distinct value in the list, or None when there are fewer than two distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly identifies the second largest distinct value, but could be more explicitly clear about handling duplicate values.
trace: To ensure that each value is counted only once, I converted the input list into a set to remove duplicates and sorted it. This allows us to find the second last element in the sorted list, which represents the second largest distinct value. An edge case handled here is when there are fewer than two unique values, in which case the function returns None.
